summary: The vulnerability analysis confirms that the plugin public file serving endpoint in packages/server/routes/plugins.js has incomplete path traversal protection. The sanitization only removes '../' sequences from the start of the path, allowing attackers to use embedded '../' sequences to escape the intended directory and read arbitrary files on the server.
expected_security_impact: An unauthenticated attacker should be able to craft a malicious URL with path traversal sequences (e.g., /public/plugin/../../../etc/passwd) to read arbitrary files on the server filesystem, including sensitive configuration files, credentials, and private keys.
actual_observed_result: Static analysis of the vulnerable code in packages/server/routes/plugins.js lines 1220-1249 confirmed the incomplete sanitization. The code uses path.normalize() and removes only leading '../' sequences with regex that only matches at the start, which fails to prevent embedded traversal patterns. The vulnerable code path is reachable via the /public/:plugin/*filepath endpoint without authentication.
verification_method: Code analysis verified that: 1) The endpoint is publicly accessible without authentication, 2) User input from req.params.filepath flows directly to the path construction, 3) The sanitization regex only matches '../' at the start of the string (^ anchor), 4) Path normalization alone is insufficient to prevent traversal when combined with path.join(), 5) The resulting file path is read and served to the user without additional validation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Sanitization regex only removes leading '../' sequences
No authentication or authorization checks on the public file endpoint
File content is read and returned to the attacker via res.sendFile() or similar
limitations_or_blockers: Full dynamic exploitation requires a running Saltcorn server with plugins configured
The PoC demonstrates the vulnerability through static code analysis and attack vector identification

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh analyzes the target repository at /target-repo to demonstrate the vulnerable code path and attack vector
